key_findings.most_important_param: reward_type is overwhelmingly most important (37.2%) — 'sharpe' >> 'return' for DQN
key_findings.small_network_wins: hidden_size=64 consistently outperforms 128/256 for discrete DQN (opposite of PPO)
key_findings.very_low_lr: Optimal lr=2.1e-5, much lower than Nature DQN 1e-4 and PPO 3.5e-4
key_findings.high_gamma: gamma=0.996 — DQN benefits from longer-horizon discount than PPO (0.982)
key_findings.small_buffer: buffer_size=17K — much smaller than FinRL 50K; fresh data matters more
key_findings.dqn_outperforms_ppo: DQN Sharpe 1.031 >> PPO Sharpe 0.485 on validation set
key_findings.consistent_high_performance: 20 out of 35 DQN trials achieved Sharpe > 0.5

literature_justification.learning_rate: Nature DQN: 1e-4. Optuna found 2.1e-5 — 5x lower for noisy financial data.
literature_justification.gamma: Standard: 0.99. Optuna found 0.996 — DQN benefits from very high discount.
literature_justification.buffer_size: FinRL DDPG: 50K. Optuna: 17K — smaller buffer keeps data fresh in non-stationary markets.
literature_justification.target_update_interval: Arxiv 2411.07585: 1000. Optuna: 679 — more frequent target updates.
literature_justification.exploration_fraction: Moderate 0.3 — extended exploration for non-stationary markets.
literature_justification.reward_type: Sharpe-based reward dominates (37% importance). Moody & Saffell (1998 NeurIPS) confirmed.
literature_justification.total_timesteps: Previous 50K was far too low. Optuna: 400K — 8x increase.

sources: Mnih et al. (2015) — Human-level Control Through Deep RL (Nature DQN)
Arxiv 2411.07585 — RL Framework for Quantitative Trading
Moody & Saffell (1998 NeurIPS) — Reinforcement Learning for Trading
